mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead. Ideal for teams looking to manage AI agents in production environments efficiently.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is essential for managing the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> Install the CLI for Linux systems.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Applies monitoring configuration.
> Applies autoscaling configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment kagenti --all-namespaces

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command will remove all deployments related to Kagenti.
> This command will delete the entire Kubernetes cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current configurations. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/kagenti.yaml

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration or missing resources.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or resize existing nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the image is not available or there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check your network connectivity.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release of Kagenti with support for AI agent orchestration and standardized communication protocols.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this initial release.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
